_doc: Single source of truth for atom-built 9-slice frames. Read by the Node bake (scripts/nine-slice-kit.mjs), the in-app editor (NineSliceEditor), and the catalog edit-link (AssetLibraryStudio). Add a frame here and it becomes bakeable, editable, and catalog-linked with no other code change. atoms = names in public/assets/ui/kit/atoms; variants[].out = composed PNG in public/assets/ui/kit; swap = palette name; consume = the CSS var its content drives; carve = flood navy bleed outside the rail; line = filename of the transparent-interior 'line' twin baked into public/assets/ui/explore/frames (full ornament over a see-through center — assembled from the corner+edge atoms with a transparent fill; no masking, no carve — the fix for the 9-slice fill problem so a surface shows behind the chrome while the frame stays; the ornament must reach the element edge, so only bracket frames qualify, NOT the inset steel row rail; see ADR-0034). lineTones = semantic-accent twins of `line`: each { out, swap } re-bakes the line frame with a palette swap applied to the corner/edge atoms, so the warm gold bracket ramp becomes a success/warning/error/warm accent while the cool steel rail is untouched — one frame character, corner accent per tone (baked beside panel-line.png, parity-tested like every other output). kind = 'junction' marks a rail junction DERIVED from the corner atom by mirroring it into each concave angle (composeTee/composeCross): tee = 3-way (sides NSE = corner + flipV(corner)), cross = 4-way (sides NSEW = corner in all four quadrants). kind = 'bar' marks a 1-D horizontal separator (panel-divider): buildBar runs the edge atom horizontally as a branch rail and seats a full 3-way tee (composeTee) at each end, so it tees into a frame's side rail as the frame's OWN corner, just branched — pixel-consistent by construction, no forged junction atom (ADR-0012's mirror-a-corner generalized from a box to interior junctions). Both are composed straight from atoms (no per-corner config); full-corner size makes the divider ~45px tall. Consumed via a horizontal 1-D border-image (`0 capW 0 capW fill`, the 90°-rotate of the scrollbar slice), NOT a 4-corner border-image; capW = barCapWidth(id). A bar carries only an integer barScale (rail weight; 1 = the frame's native rail), so it is excluded from the 4-corner 9-slice editor and calibrated as a live divider-in-panel preview instead.

tee.label: Rail junction — 3-way tee (derived from the corner atom)

cross.label: Rail junction — 4-way cross (derived from the corner atom)
